title: Forced to Wear a Burqa in Kabul: My Life Just Flipped! 😭 #shorts #storytime
script: Mandatory burqa rules in Kabul: my life just flipped upside down.  I'm in Kabul, and they're making us wear burqas every time we step outside. My family's arguing nonstop about it. My mom says it's for our safety, but I feel like I'm disappearing. At school, my friends and I whisper about how unfair it is, but we're all scared. I mean, what if we get caught without one?   Every morning, I stand in front of the mirror, looking at the burqa, feeling trapped. My dad says we have to follow the rules, but I can't shake this feeling of defiance. It's like my identity is being erased. I hear stories of girls getting in trouble, and it terrifies me.   One day, I decide enough is enough. title: Is Cultural Sharing a Crime Now? 😭 Let's Embrace Traditions! #shorts #muslim
script: Why is it that sharing cultures is seen as a crime now? Wallah, it’s just respect and love, not appropriation!  I’m seriously confused, bro. Like, why do people think cultural sharing is some kind of crime? My friends, who aren’t even Muslim, join me for iftar during Ramadan. They show up, break fast with me, and it’s all about solidarity and respect. But some people are out here calling it appropriation? Astaghfirullah, that’s just ridiculous!  And get this, my black friends used to braid my hair. We were celebrating their culture, and it was awesome! But now, people act like appreciating someone else’s traditions is a bad thing. title: True Hero Naeem Rashid's Sacrifice Leaves Us Heartbroken 😭🙏 #shorts #muslim
script: Can you believe a true hero, who risked everything to protect his community, has left us? Wallah, it’s beyond heartbreaking!  Naeem Rashid, man... He stood up to a shooter at a mosque with his bare hands. It's like, how do you even process that kind of bravery? As a Muslim teen, this hits hard. I mean, places of worship should be safe, right? But now, we’re all just feeling this fear creeping in.  Losing someone like Rashid isn’t just about losing a life. It’s like losing hope in a world where we should all feel safe, yallah. His courage is the strength we need, and it’s just... gone.  How do we even move forward when heroes like him are taken from us? Inshallah, we find a way. But right now, I’m just stuck on this feeling of loss.  Subscribe before I get banned!

title: Why Being Muslim in 2026 Feels Like a Crime 😭 #muslim #shorts
script: Why does being a Muslim in 2026 still feel like a crime?  Every day, I'm out here just trying to live my life, wallah, and it feels like I'm constantly under attack. I'm seeing my brothers and sisters face mob violence, and it's just so exhausting. Like, why is this our reality?  Mosques are being burned, my friends can't even get jobs because of their faith, and harassment in school is just... normal now. It's like fear is the new normal, and it's heartbreaking. I just want to live my faith without looking over my shoulder every second.  Yallah, can we find a place where we can just be ourselves? Inshallah, things will change, but right now, it feels like a never-ending struggle. Subscribe before I get banned!

title: Stabbed During Prayer?! This Mosque Betrayal is UNREAL! 😭🙏 #shorts #islam
script: Can you believe someone pretended to pray just to stab a brother in a mosque?  Wallah, this is beyond sickening! I'm sitting here, trying to wrap my head around how someone could betray such a sacred space. This brother was in the middle of prostration, the ultimate act of faith, and he gets stabbed, like, 50 times! Astaghfir, the sheer hatred behind that... it’s just unreal.   He died repeating praises to Allah, and now our community is left shattered. This isn’t just a random act of violence. It's a dark reminder of the racism and intolerance we face every single day. Yallah, how can we let hate win like this?  Subscribe before I get banned!

title: 2026 & We're Still Facing This Hate?! 😭🙏 #shorts #muslim
script: How are we living in 2026 and still facing this kind of hatred? Wallah, it's like history is repeating itself!  I just heard about this terrorist attack where a bunch of tourists got killed. title: Why Do They Lie About Islam? 🤔 Unpacking Dangerous Myths! #shorts #muslim
script: Why do people insist on lying about Islam?  So today I come across this video claiming honor killing is legal and blaming Islam for it. Wallah, the more I listen, the more my blood boils. People agreeing with this nonsense, throwing around "religion of peace" like they know what they're talking about. It's dangerous, bro. This kind of misinformation can mess with people's faith, make them doubt Islam, or push them away.   Why do people talk about Islam when they have zero clue?   Subscribe before I get banned!

title: Why Are We Responding to Hate with Hate? 😭🙏 #muslim #shorts
script: Why are we, as Muslims, sinking to the same level as those who hate us?  Wallah, I'm scrolling through my feed and I see some Muslims responding to Islamophobia with the same hate. It's like, why are we doing this? It’s frustrating because it goes against everything we’re taught. Our Prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him, showed patience and compassion even when facing the worst. So why are we acting like this?  I get it, the trolls are everywhere, but barking back just messes with our mental health. It's exhausting and pointless. We should be focusing on improving ourselves, not wasting energy on these people.  Yallah, we need to rise above and show the world who we really are. We have to unite and stand strong. If the whole ummah is solid, no one can stop us.   Subscribe before I get banne

title: Are Muslims Being Hypocritical? This is Frustrating, Wallah! 😭 #shorts #muslim
script: Are Muslims being super racist right now?  Lately, I've been feeling so frustrated, wallah. Some Muslims are distancing themselves from "brown" people, and it feels hypocritical and wrong. Like, how can you stand against Islamophobia and then turn around and act like being brown is a problem?  I see these prominent Muslims condemning racism, but then they're quick to say, "Not all of us are like that," as if being brown is something to be ashamed of. It's like they're throwing their own brothers and sisters under the bus. This disassociation feels like a betrayal, astaghfir.  Why are we, as Muslims, suddenly acting like our skin color defines our faith? Yallah, can we just stand together instead of throwing shade at each other? Inshallah, we need to do better! title: Stop Stereotyping Indian Guys! 😡🙏 #shorts #muslim #storytime

title: Is Going Back to Sleep After Fajr Allowed?! 🤔 #muslim #shorts
script: Is waking up for fajr and going back to sleep even allowed, bro?!  So here's the deal. I'm up for fajr, I pray, and then... I crash back into bed. Wallah, it feels like I'm just checking a box. Like, is that even okay? I mean, fajr is supposed to be this deep connection with Allah, right? But here I am, snoozing away like nothing happened.  And don't get me started on the struggle of balancing this with school. I need my sleep, but I also want to keep my prayers meaningful. It's like, how am I supposed to function when I barely get enough rest? Is this even healthy, yallah?  Y’all ever feel like fajr is just a checkbox you hit before going back to sleep?
